source.domain: pet care, veterinary clinic tools, animal mobility aids, grooming equipment, and non-implant animal-care hardware

requirements.part_name: Adjustable Canine Rehabilitation Harness Frame - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Adjustable Canine Rehabilitation Harness Frame'.
The broader use case is: A veterinary physical therapy aid used to support dogs recovering from leg
injuries during treadmill or walking exercises. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body
implied by: Machined aluminum sliding side rail with indexed height holes and clamp slots All other
product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this
mechanical role: Provides adjustable torso support points so a therapist can partially unload weight
from the animal's limbs. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Requires
long extrusions, repeated hole patterns, elongated slots, rounded safety edges, and precise
alignment between adjustable clamp features. The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or
equivalent using rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw
oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and
edge treatments into that same solid piece.

functional_features.name: single rectangular metal body
functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 180 mm x 110 mm x 26 mm.

functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern
functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 12 mm on the length centerline at X=22 mm and X=158 mm, Y=55 mm.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 60 mm long x 11 mm wide through the part, centered at X=90 mm, Y=55 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 45 mm x 36 mm x 9 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

tolerances: Overall envelope dimensions: +/-0.20 mm.
Hole diameters and slot widths: +/-0.10 mm.
Hole and slot center positions from datums B and C: +/-0.15 mm.
Pocket depths and relief depths: +/-0.10 mm.
Nonfunctional chamfers and radii: +/-0.30 mm.
acceptance_checklist: The output contains one continuous metal solid representing one part.
The stock family, material intent, envelope dimensions, and datum orientation match this requirement.
The feature named 'single rectangular metal body' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'mounting hole pattern' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'functional center feature' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'top relief pocket' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'edge chamfers' is present with the stated size and position.
All required chamfers, reliefs, and edge treatments are visible in STEP and STL outputs.
No extra parts, fasteners, hardware, labels, cosmetic meshes, or nonmetal components are present.
Flat datums remain planar and usable; pockets or slots do not accidentally break unsupported walls.
negative_requirements: Represent exactly one metal part only.
Do not model screws, nuts, springs, bushings, bearings, rubber pads, plastic covers, electronics, labels, handles, knobs, adhesives, weld beads, or separate inserts.
Do not convert the part into an assembly or multiple bodies.
Do not replace through holes, slots, pockets, grooves, flats, tapers, or chamfers with cosmetic markings.
Do not omit datum-critical features just because they are small.
Do not broaden the requirement back into the full product idea named 'Adjustable Canine Rehabilitation Harness Frame'; this requirement is only for its chosen metal part.
requirements.immutable_requirement_note: After review, this English requirement is the product truth for later SubCAD code generation. Do not simplify it to fit current SubCAD limitations.

source.domain: pet care, veterinary clinic tools, animal mobility aids, grooming equipment, and non-implant animal-care hardware

requirements.part_name: Folding Pet Grooming Arm Clamp - single metal part

material.reason: durable wear surface for workshop loading

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the cylinder axis as X. The left faced end is datum A at X=0. The radial direction through the largest flat or slot is datum B. Positive Z points upward from the part centerline when the main flat faces up.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Folding Pet Grooming Arm Clamp'. The broader use
case is: A grooming-table attachment that holds a leash loop above the table while allowing the arm
to fold flat for storage. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: CNC-machined
hinge clamp block with table jaw, pivot bore, and locking pin channel All other product elements are
external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Locks
a vertical grooming arm securely to a table edge and releases it into a folded transport position.
It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Combines jaw geometry, hinge
bosses, coaxial bores, pin clearances, chamfers, and load-bearing wall thickness constraints. The
part is made from 1045 medium-carbon steel, normalized using round bar stock. Start from one cut
length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary
flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the
deliverable.

functional_features.name: single cylindrical body
functional_features.description: Turn one coaxial cylinder to OD 85 mm and length 26 mm from solid round bar.

functional_features.name: axial through bore
functional_features.description: Machine a centered through bore diameter 17 mm along the full length; both bore mouths have 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers.

functional_features.name: end counterbores
functional_features.description: Add shallow concentric counterbores on both ends, diameter 27 mm x 3 mm deep.

functional_features.name: milled reference flat
functional_features.description: Mill one longitudinal flat 28 mm wide over 18 mm of length, centered on the top side.

functional_features.name: radial clamp holes
functional_features.description: Add two radial tapped M8 holes through the top flat at X=8 mm and X=17 mm.

functional_features.name: split relief slit
functional_features.description: Cut one full-length radial slit 4 mm wide from the outside to the axial bore on datum-B side.

functional_features.name: outside edge treatment
functional_features.description: Break all outside circular edges with 1.0 mm chamfers and keep internal bore edges visibly chamfered.

tolerances: Outside diameter and axial bore diameter: +/-0.05 mm.
Concentricity of bore to outer diameter: within 0.05 mm TIR.
Milled flats and slots: +/-0.15 mm unless otherwise specified.
Nonfunctional chamfers: +/-0.3 mm.
acceptance_checklist: The output contains one continuous metal solid representing one part.

The feature named 'split relief slit' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'outside edge treatment' is present with the stated size and position.

No extra parts, fasteners, hardware, labels, cosmetic meshes, or nonmetal components are present.

Do not convert the part into an assembly or multiple bodies.

source.raw_idea_title: Veterinary Exam Table Paw Positioning Fixture

requirements.part_name: Veterinary Exam Table Paw Positioning Fixture - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Veterinary Exam Table Paw Positioning Fixture'. The
broader use case is: A clinic tool used to gently position an animal's paw during wound checks, nail
work, or imaging prep. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined contoured
paw cradle plate with radial angle slots and mounting holes All other product elements are external
reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Cradles and
indexes a paw at repeatable angles without compressing soft tissue. It is intentionally included in
the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests organic-adjacent concave surfaces, radial slot layouts,
filleted edges, countersunk fasteners, and ergonomic clearance zones. The part is made from low-
carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using sheet or plate stock. Start from one flat sheet or plate
blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that
same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded
pieces.

functional_features.name: single rectangular metal body
functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 240 mm x 110 mm x 4 mm.

functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern
functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 7 mm on the length centerline at X=22 mm and X=218 mm, Y=55 mm.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 80 mm long x 16 mm wide through the part, centered at X=120 mm, Y=55 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 60 mm x 36 mm x 2 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

functional_features.name: machined angled reference face
functional_features.description: Machine one top reference face at 26 degrees over the last 48 mm of length.

Pocket depths and relief depths: +/-0.10 mm.

requirements.part_name: Small Animal Oxygen Mask Manifold - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Small Animal Oxygen Mask Manifold'. The broader use
case is: A non-invasive veterinary respiratory accessory for directing oxygen into interchangeable
masks for cats, rabbits, or small dogs. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by:
Machined polymer manifold body with hose barb port, gasket groove, and fastener bosses All other
product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this
mechanical role: Splits and regulates airflow from a hose connection into a mask interface with
leak-resistant mounting. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Requires
internal flow passages, sealing grooves, threaded or barbed connectors, boss reinforcement, and
smooth transitions between cylindrical and rectangular features. The part is made from low-carbon
steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block
or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces,
grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 36 mm long x 15 mm wide through the part, centered at X=55 mm, Y=42 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 27 mm x 28 mm x 6 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

functional_features.name: side tapped hole
functional_features.description: Tap one side hole M5 from the right long edge into the central pocket; hole axis is parallel to Y.

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Canine Wheelchair Rear Axle Mount'. The broader use
case is: A mobility aid component for dogs with rear-leg weakness, connecting the frame tubes to the
rear wheels. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined billet axle bracket
with tube sockets, axle bore, and stepped adjustment holes All other product elements are external
reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Holds wheel
axle position while allowing height and stance-width adjustment for different dog sizes. It is
intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests intersecting cylindrical sockets,
offset bores, mirrored bracket geometry, structural ribs, and parametric hole spacing. The part is
made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using round bar stock. Start from one cut length
of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats,
slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

case is: A grooming accessory fitted around a rotary nail grinder to control how much claw reaches
the abrasive wheel. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined guard ring
with stepped nail ports and grinder collar interface All other product elements are external
reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Limits grinding
depth and provides different openings for small, medium, and large pet nails. It is intentionally
included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Includes concentric ring geometry, asymmetric access
ports, thin protective walls, bevels, snap-fit features, and size-indexed cutouts. The part is made
from 1045 medium-carbon steel, normalized using round bar stock. Start from one cut length of round

radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

source.domain: pet care, veterinary clinic tools, animal mobility aids, grooming equipment, and non-implant animal-care hardware
source.raw_idea_title: Pet Bath Spray Nozzle Wall Dock

requirements.part_name: Pet Bath Spray Nozzle Wall Dock - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Pet Bath Spray Nozzle Wall Dock'. The broader use
case is: A grooming-room fixture for holding a handheld spray nozzle and routing hose strain relief
near a wash tub. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined wall-mounted
saddle bracket with curved nozzle cradle and hose guide channel All other product elements are
external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role:
Secures the nozzle between uses and prevents hose kinking or dropping into the tub. It is
intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Requires curved cradles, wall fastener
countersinks, undercut-like retention lips, blended hose channels, and water-shedding surfaces. The
part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using sheet or plate stock. Start from
one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and
relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet
part, not separate welded pieces.

functional_features.name: single rectangular metal body
functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 235 mm x 50 mm x 4 mm.

functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern
functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 9 mm on the length centerline at X=10 mm and X=225 mm, Y=25 mm.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 78 mm long x 17 mm wide through the part, centered at X=117 mm, Y=25 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 58 mm x 18 mm x 1 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

tolerances: Overall envelope dimensions: +/-0.20 mm.
Hole diameters and slot widths: +/-0.10 mm.
Hole and slot center positions from datums B and C: +/-0.15 mm.
Pocket depths and relief depths: +/-0.10 mm.
Nonfunctional chamfers and radii: +/-0.30 mm.

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.

requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Animal Recovery Cone Quick-Release Hinge'. The
broader use case is: A reusable e-collar hardware upgrade for clinics that need fast removal and
reattachment during wound inspection. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by:
Machined hinge latch plate with living-clearance knuckle bores and spring latch pocket All other
product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this
mechanical role: Lets a protective cone open and close around an animal's neck without threading
tabs each time. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests small hinge
knuckles, alternating bore alignment, latch detents, thin plate features, rounded corners, and
assembly clearances. The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using sheet or
plate stock. Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes,
slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are
bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.

requirements.part_name: Clinic Kennel Door Bowl Lock - single metal part

material.reason: corrosion resistance and cleanable metal surfaces

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Clinic Kennel Door Bowl Lock'. The broader use case
is: A veterinary boarding accessory that locks a removable food or water bowl to the inside of a
kennel door. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined stainless locking
bracket with bowl rim capture groove and quarter-turn latch boss All other product elements are
external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role:
Prevents animals from tipping the bowl while allowing staff to remove it quickly for cleaning. It is
intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Combines sheet-like bracket geometry,
circular rim channels, latch rotation stops, sanitary radii, and corrosion-resistant fastener
detailing. The part is made from AISI 316 stainless steel using sheet or plate stock. Start from one
flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief
features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not
separate welded pieces.
